creator: marius@alchemy.franken.de
text:
On Sat, Oct 16, 2010 at 10:11:15PM +0300, Aldis Berjoza wrote:
> >The main problem according to your log seems to be that headers within
> >the ${WRKSRC} aren't found:
> >        ==> MAKING DEPENDENCIES "OBJ/amd64-freebsd-cc/align_test.d"
> >makedepend: warning:  align_test.c, line 2: cannot find include file  
> >"schily/mconfig.h"
> ><...>
> >align_test.c:24:26: error: schily/stdio.h: No such file or directory
> >align_test.c:25:29: error: schily/standard.h: No such file or directory
> >align_test.c:27:27: error: schily/utypes.h: No such file or directory
> ><...>
> >
> >I suspect the issue triggering this is that the port auto-detects it's
> >being built on amd64 rather than the desired i386:
> >        ==> MAKING SYMLINKS in ./RULES/^M
> >sh conf/cc-config.sh cc cc incs/Dcc.amd64-freebsd
> ><...>
> 
> yup, I also noticed that
> 
> >If some other part of the build thinks it's built on i386 that IMO
> >would very well explain the above as cdrtools uses all kinds of
> >symlinks etc containing the arch. What mechanism are you using in
> >order to let uname(1) report i386 instead of amd64? I suspect this
> >might require 8/stable as of r210855 (src/sys/kern/kern_mib.c rev
> >1.98.2.5) or later to work seemlessly.
> 
> I use very primitive method of:
> setenv MACHINE i386
> setenv UNAME_p i386
> setenv UNAME_m i386
> 
> in /etc/csh.cshrc
> so each time i jexec to jail, they ENV is ready
> 

Ok, so the problem most likely is that the port sets up a pristine
environment using `env -i` in order to allow i386 releases to be
cross-built on amd64, which requires an amd64 mkisofs built as
part of the release build. Your patch bypasses that again so the
full environment is inherited. Given that cross-building i386
releases on amd64 using native binaries and building i386 binaries
on amd64 using i386 binaries are somewhat contradicting goals the
only viable solution I currently see is to use stable/8 as of
r210855 or later so uname(1) will report i386 as the architecture
on amd64 regardless of the environment (apart from fixing the
release build scripts to not set TARGET and TARGET_ARCH when
cross-building, which might not easily possible as it at least
can't be done here via `env -i` either or else building i386
releases in a jail on amd64 again will suffer the same problem...)

> >I've spotted no case where ${LOCALBASE}/include (${PREFIX} is wrong
> >here) would be missing but I could have overlooked it.
> >
> >Marius
> >
> 
> btw, cdrtools v2 compiles just fine

Well, this doesn't mean much as the build framework has changed
quite a bit since then.

Marius
